title: SPLIT Rei Shinji relationship portrayal in 222
display_title: SPLIT Rei Shinji relationship portrayal in 222
wikitext:
[]: SPLIT: Rei/Shinji relationship portrayal in 2.22             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Apr 21, 2011 2:44 pm Split from this thread .~ SSD This is off-topic, but really: i have read numerous times that the movie is about the Shinji/Rei relationship, and it baffles me to no end. I guess people assume that if two young characters are involved in what could be called a rudimentary romance, then that's what the film is about despite the fact that: a, 80% of the time (rough estimate) when he is on screen, Shinji is shown to be ponderous/hopeful/furious about/towards Gendo. Even during the ultimate climax, he is thanking Rei for her efforts to try and bring them together. b, As a a matter of fact, every major dramatic event in the movie involving Shinji revolves mostly around Gendo (who's the first person he runs to before battling Zeruel and, again, who's the lat person he mentions?). c, Rei herself gains prominnence in Shinji's life mostly through the backdoor of the latter's daddy issues. Pretty thick shipping googles are required to call this anything but a father and son movie (the father-son conflict being presented in a mind numbingly tedious fashion while sacrificing potentially interesting character arcs in the process, but whatever).

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Thu Apr 21, 2011 2:51 pm I think that people are screwing up on the idea of a "relationship". In Rebuild, the main focus is between both father and son, but that isn't what a relationship (in this situation) is considered. Saying it is a Gendo/Shinji relationship makes it sound like Gendo is going to ask Shinji out. The relationship is a R/S relationship. But, this isn't the focus in the movie. Hope this clears up the confusion on this topic. Edited at request of the Admin.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Thu Apr 21, 2011 3:02 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Thu Apr 21, 2011 3:05 pm Gendo and Shinji's storyline (in this film) is mostly a means to the end of furthering the Rei/Shinji arc. I think Shinji can appreciate the intent behind Rei's wanting to bring him and his father together, even if he thinks it's a bad idea. "It's the thought that counts" and all that. Given the final scene, I don't see how this isn't an R/S movie. I'd compare it to the Lord of the Rings films... characters are emphasized and pushed back as the plot requires. Gandalf is mostly gone in The Two Towers, but is featured prominently in the first and third films. Frodo (definitely the main character) takes a back seat in the second film so Aragorn can be established. I'm OK with Asuka being a background player for now. Given the events of 2.X, it seems like her story is about to heat up in a big way...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 21, 2011 3:11 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: is mentioned as a sibling in one of the key new inclusions in 1.11,

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Thu Apr 21, 2011 3:20 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Apr 21, 2011 3:33 pm Warren Peace wrote: Gendo and Shinji's storyline (in this film) is mostly a means to the end of furthering the Rei/Shinji arc. I think Shinji can appreciate the intent behind Rei's wanting to bring him and his father together, even if he thinks it's a bad idea. "It's the thought that counts" and all that. Given the final scene, I don't see how this isn't an R/S movie.

[]:             Quote Post by Hiten777 » Fri Apr 22, 2011 2:28 am I agree with Stryker. I think a few people are mistaking the roles of relationships in 2.22. Some seem to think that there can't be more than one relationship and that the main focal relationship puts to rest any chance of other relationships having equal or more significance. I do agree that Gendo-Shinji clashing is one of the main focal points of the movies, but I would say that the main point is Shinji's internal battle. Mostly Shinji is dealing more with his conflicted views on his father rather than directly with Gendo. He struggles between "How do I get my father to acknowledge me?" and "Do I really want to get to know the man who threw me away?" Personally, I believe the S/R relationship is what most would consider the implied pairing of the movie. Many would say its not a pairing because is lacks most of the pairing necessities, but to Eva fans who know how psycho analytical Eva is it's given enough signs to be a leading part. Rei's part in Shinji's life seems to be the type of girl who is willing to go out of her comfort zone to make him happy without expecting anything in return, while Shinji has proven (by ending in 2.22) that he sees her for herself (not able to be replaced) and is willing to risk anything to protect her. Just a small bit for those who argue the Asuka/Shinji shipping in rebuild. I won't argue that Asuka/Shinji won't happen or will happen later on since obviously I have no idea what'll happen. But Asuka doesn't seem to have much impact on the story romantically as she did in the series. Sure she acknowledges that she likes Shinji but it's also implied that she stepped aside in choosing to pilot Unit 03. I see her as more of a step-sister type character to Shinji since there may be attraction and jealousy due to competing against another female for attention.

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Fri Apr 22, 2011 11:08 am Well, yeah. I generally agree that nothing we've seen between Shinji and Rei so far is necessarily romantic. I wouldn't exactly be shocked if the last two movies settled them into a cozy brother-sister dynamic. But if that were the case, I would seriously question the wisdom of spending so much time in 2.x on a subplot involving misunderstood romantic intentions... The cook-off clearly had the trappings of a love triangle, and I don't understand what point there would have been to establishing it if it was just fake-out.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Fri Apr 22, 2011 11:28 am BornIn1142 wrote: Well, yeah. I generally agree that nothing we've seen between Shinji and Rei so far is necessarily romantic.

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Fri Apr 22, 2011 11:47 am I said " necessarily romantic." Obviously, their relationship has a pretty strong romantic overtone and that's why most people who've seen the movie have taken it as such. That's what I'm defaulting to. However, at the end of the day, all that we've seen between them is unspecific affection, and considering the obvious familial element between them, I think it would be rather easy to interpret that affection as platonic. Furthermore, since neither of them is exactly good at socializing, I don't think normal social cues for crushes are the ultimate proof of anything here. Also, I completely disagree with your notion that saving someone's life is only done between men or lovers.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri Apr 22, 2011 11:59 am Hiten777 wrote: I agree with Stryker. I think a few people are mistaking the roles of relationships in 2.22. Some seem to think that there can't be more than one relationship and that the main focal relationship puts to rest any chance of other relationships having equal or more significance.

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Fri Apr 22, 2011 12:20 pm SaltyJoe wrote: I would say the there is a very clear romantic current... on Rei's part. Not so much from Shinji, final scene or no.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Fri Apr 22, 2011 12:22 pm BornIn1142 wrote: Also, I completely disagree with your notion that saving someone's life is only done between men or lovers.

[]:             Quote Post by Hiten777 » Fri Apr 22, 2011 2:28 pm I think people are getting things majorly confused on certain issues. The part about saving people is the feeling of "nakama." Most already know that's a term that English can't really put into specific words. In other words it's like saying "I see this person as my family or special and I would go out of my way to help them." This doesn't have to mean family love or romantic love but looking out for each other. Also, I hear the "Rei is Shinji's mother" line played so much it's getting old. To say she's his mother means that she would have to have both Yui's body AND soul, which is incorrect. She physically resembles Yui on the outside but has the soul of Lilith. Everyone knows that Yui is in Eva 01 and she really can't be in two places at once. Also what Rei feels doesn't really have to be solely because she share something with Yui, she does her actions spontaneously, like the whole cloth ringing thing. But to say that she sees her love for Shinji as only maternal is like saying you can only feel 1 thing and nothing else at the same time. Maternal instinct doesn't come from just having been a mother, its a natural instinct to feel when you want to protect someone or something. It's easy for someone to want to care for someone maternally and yet be in love with them. Like a girl who comforts the boy she likes during a time of vulnerability. Shinji's saving of Rei doesn't mean he felt mostly grief for not being able to protect Asuka. If that were the case then wouldn't he feel the same way about protecting Misato and the rest since they were also in danger. Asuka (like Touji) served as the reason for Shinji quitting to pilot, at that time he didn't want to pilto again for any reason, even after knowing how damaging Zeruel was. It wasn't until he saw that Rei was taken that he finally reflected on his actions. Asuka's fate gave him something to reflect on and gave him to courage to do what he wanted, to save another person close to him. He seemed even more determined to save Rei since he decided to destroy everything if it meant rescuing her alone. That alone shows that he care for her to a very high degree.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Apr 22, 2011 2:31 pm I'm not saying that the Rebuild movies are a series of romance dramas or anything - Of COURSE its about Shinji and Gendo, no one here disputed that but the little shipping stuff that is in there is mostly R/S. Misato calls it love, Asuka calls it love, Kensuke goes "I'm so jealous!" and while none of them explicitly said "I wanna fuck you", their interactions (and not just the interactions themselves but the way they are carried out - the accentuating of each little smile, the slow, hesitantly spoken words) would be seen as typical romance material in Anime(exept hentai and stuff, most of it works with this vagueness - including old NGE with the S/A stuff.), and in fact, 14 year olds don't do much except holding hands, smiling at each other, hugging and stuff. (They DO "experiment" but seldom with those they have serious crushes on) Also, they properly hold hands i 1.X Shinji's not pulling her out of the plug, he stepped into it (I'm not sure whether he did that in the original), they're just holding hands. Yes, friends and family also hold hands, but it's not the family/friend-ish *grabs hand* "I'm there for you" thing, it's the "tenderly placing the hands upon each other" thing.) If it was just the cooking, or just Asuka's words, or just the hand-holding... yeah, too far-fetched. But it's all that stuff at once. Most new wievers (which Rebuild was supposed to attract) would see this as obvious Official Couple material. - Yeah, they can still subvert it and stuff, but to say that us R/S fans are just seeing things that are not there is simply not right. If several characters in the actual movie (including the potential Rival) explicitly call it love and the characters have interactions that could be seen as typical ship teasing, it is NOT far fetched to say they're in love. We're NOT seeing or interpreting to much into things, we just chose to interpret the things we see in another, also legitimate way.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Fri Apr 22, 2011 8:16 pm Hiten777 wrote: Also, I hear the "Rei is Shinji's mother" line played so much it's getting old. To say she's his mother means that she would have to have both Yui's body AND soul, which is incorrect. She physically resembles Yui on the outside but has the soul of Lilith. Everyone knows that Yui is in Eva 01 and she really can't be in two places at once. Also what Rei feels doesn't really have to be solely because she share something with Yui, she does her actions spontaneously, like the whole cloth ringing thing. But to say that she sees her love for Shinji as only maternal is like saying you can only feel 1 thing and nothing else at the same time. Maternal instinct doesn't come from just having been a mother, its a natural instinct to feel when you want to protect someone or something. It's easy for someone to want to care for someone maternally and yet be in love with them. Like a girl who comforts the boy she likes during a time of vulnerability.

[]:             Quote Post by Hiten777 » Fri Apr 22, 2011 8:41 pm Warren Peace wrote: No no, she doesn't just resemble Yui physically, her body is Yui's body . That's an important distinction. IF Rei is fully aware of her origins (like she was in NGE) and she has romantic intentions towards Shinji, she's morally obligated to inform him of this fact.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 22, 2011 9:40 pm Er, I'm gonna be hitting a lot of previously established points from other threads and I hope I'm not bringing things somehow off-topic. I just felt they should be mentioned in this thread. BornIn1142 wrote: The cook-off clearly had the trappings of a love triangle, and I don't understand what point there would have been to establishing it if it was just fake-out.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sat Apr 23, 2011 5:59 am Hiten777 wrote: Actually, not really to that extent. Her body is made up of the remains (they never stated how much of Yui was left, most likely just cells after she was absorbed) of Yui's DNA and Lilith's DNA as well. So she's not really a Yui body, just a combination of the two. Her body was created not reused using Yui's body, there's a difference. I don't think that warrens her to say "just letting you know, I'm actually part of your mother." Because of her genetics it would make more sense to liken her to a relative and not the same exact person, which would be more of the case if they ONLY used Yui's DNA.
